diary:
Woke up in Bothell to a bright, easy Monday—sunny enough that the morning felt like it had already decided to be kind to me. After coffee, I did that slow reset where you let the day come to you before you start chasing it. Breakfast was quick at the kitchen counter, then I drove into the studio and just fell into my gameplay system. Hours flew by while I debugged level flow, chasing little friction points like they were stubborn bugs in my own patience.

Lunch at Kirkland Urban Farm Market was outside, and I actually savored it—74 degrees and all. Later I grabbed snacks and drinks at Safeway for the match, then spent the afternoon polishing UI interactions and testing across devices.

By early evening I was near Lake Union Park, and the kayak session felt like a soft reset too. Afterward, I wandered to Pike Place Market for something fast, then watched USA vs Wales at The Triple Door downtown with everyone buzzing. I’m tired in a good way—content, sun-warmed, and ready for tomorrow.
thought_process: I leaned into work early because the morning felt clear and I wanted to get the hardest debugging done before my energy started to drift. The UI polish came after errands because I needed fresh snacks and a real break to keep my focus from turning sloppy. I chose to eat outside at the Kirkland Urban Farm Market because the weather matched my mood—when it’s sunny, I’m better at not overthinking and more willing to take the “small win” breaks. The World Cup match gave the day a gentle anchor; even though it wasn’t related to my code, it made the evening feel purposeful. The light rail closure almost nudged my route planning, but driving kept things simple. My mood shifted from focused to relaxed by Lake Union, then to excited during the match—like the city’s energy synced with mine.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
